description_long: This object is a pair of compasses, commonly used for drawing circles or arcs and measuring distances on maps or technical drawings. It features two long, slender arms connected by a central pivot joint, with one arm ending in a sharp metal point and the other in a pencil or marking tip. The body is primarily constructed from metal, with a blue plastic or painted section at the pivot for grip and adjustment. The arms are straight and appear to be made of a dark, possibly anodized metal, with visible adjustment screws for fine-tuning the span. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements, focusing on precision and durability. The compasses are compact and lightweight, suitable for handheld use in urban environments such as schools, offices, or workshops.
description: A precision metal compass with straight arms, a blue pivot joint, and adjustable screws for drawing circles or arcs. The design is utilitarian and built for accuracy.
description_view_0: Front view showing the two arms spread in a V-shape, with the blue pivot joint clearly visible at the top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the arms, the pointed tips, and the thickness of the blue pivot section.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the blue pivot and the symmetrical spread of the arms.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the straight alignment of the arms and the adjustment screws.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, slender metal handles with a blue plastic grip at the pivot; not insulated or ergonomic.
attribute_jaw_design: Needle-pointed jaws, one with a sharp metal tip and the other with a pencil holder; designed for precision rather than gripping.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed central pivot with an adjustment screw for setting the arm span; does not allow for slip-joint or compound movement.
attribute_grip_pattern: Handles appear smooth with no visible texturing or ribbing; blue pivot section may provide slight tactile grip.
